hints.monumentum: 'monumentum' is a noun — the direct object
hints.aere: 'aere' is a noun in the ablative case
hints.perennius: 'perennius' is a comparative adjective — it doesn't appear in this excerpt

hints.to drive out: 'to drive out' is 'expellere' — 'Exegi' comes from 'exigere' (to complete/accomplish)
hints.to need: 'to need' could be 'egere' — but 'Exegi' comes from 'exigere'

hints.Present: Present 1st person would be 'exigo' — 'Exegi' has the perfect stem
hints.Pluperfect: Pluperfect 1st person would be 'exegeram' — 'Exegi' lacks the -era- marker

hints.ago, agere, egi, actum: These are the principal parts of 'agere' (to drive/do) — the base verb of 'exigere'
hints.lego, legere, legi, lectum: These are the principal parts of 'legere' (to read/gather)
hints.rego, regere, rexi, rectum: These are the principal parts of 'regere' (to rule)

hints.Nominative: 'monumentum' could be nominative, but here it's the direct object of 'Exegi'

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'monumentum' with -um ending is 2nd declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension nouns have varied nominatives — '-um' ending is characteristic of 2nd declension neuter
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns end in -us/-u — 'monumentum' is 2nd declension neuter

hints.Nominative: Nominative of 'aes' is 'aes' — 'aere' with -e ending is ablative

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'aes' with genitive 'aeris' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'aes' is a 3rd declension neuter noun
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es with genitive -ei — 'aes' has genitive 'aeris'

translation: I have built a monument of bronze
